description: Cross-field REACTIVE recomputation on SOURCE change (§55.11). Sequence: confirm "secret12", password "secret12" (now matching, confirm valid), then password CHANGES to "other123". Because eq(@signup.password) is a reactive consumer of password, confirm re-validates: confirm.errors becomes .EqFailed(expected="other123") and confirm.isValid flips to false — the dependent field re-checks when its SOURCE changes.
rationale: §55.11 mechanics + §55.6: 'A change to a cell referenced in a cross-field predicate arg recomputes the dependent field's surface.' This case pins that reactivity — the expected payload tracks the NEW password value (other123), proving confirm re-ran after the source changed, not a stale first evaluation.
